findings.name: URL filtering policy allows uncategorized websites
findings.description: The default URL filtering policy allows access to uncategorized websites. Newly registered or uncategorized domains are commonly used for phishing and malware distribution.
findings.recommendation: Block or caution access to uncategorized URLs. Create an exception process for legitimate uncategorized sites requiring business justification.

findings.name: URL filtering does not block newly registered domains
findings.description: No policy rule blocks access to domains registered within the last 30 days. Over 70% of phishing domains are used within 48 hours of registration.
findings.recommendation: Add a URL filtering rule to block domains registered within the last 30 days. Create an exception workflow for legitimate newly registered domains.

findings.name: Social media file sharing not restricted
findings.description: URL filtering allows full access to social media platforms without restricting file upload/download capabilities. This enables data exfiltration via social channels.
findings.recommendation: Apply granular social media controls: allow browsing but block file uploads. Use cloud application controls for platform-specific restrictions.

findings.name: SSL inspection not enabled for all traffic
findings.description: SSL inspection is enabled for only 45% of web traffic. Categories excluded from inspection include financial services, healthcare, and miscellaneous. Threats in encrypted traffic go undetected.
findings.recommendation: Enable SSL inspection for all traffic categories. Add only legally required exemptions (e.g., banking sites in regulated regions). Deploy the Zscaler root CA to all endpoints.

findings.name: SSL inspection certificate uses SHA-1 signature
findings.description: The SSL inspection CA certificate uses SHA-1 for its signature algorithm. SHA-1 is cryptographically weak and deprecated by all major browsers.
findings.recommendation: Regenerate the SSL inspection CA certificate with SHA-256 or SHA-384 signature. Redistribute the new CA to all managed endpoints.

findings.name: SSL inspection bypass for cloud storage services
findings.description: Cloud storage services (Google Drive, OneDrive, Dropbox, Box) are excluded from SSL inspection. These are common vectors for malware delivery and data exfiltration.
findings.recommendation: Enable SSL inspection for cloud storage services. Use CASB inline policies for granular upload/download controls on inspected traffic.

findings.name: DLP policies not covering PII patterns
findings.description: DLP engines are active but no policies are configured for personally identifiable information (PII) patterns such as SSN, passport numbers, or national IDs.
findings.recommendation: Configure DLP dictionaries and engines for PII detection: SSN (US), national IDs, passport numbers, date of birth patterns. Apply to web and cloud app traffic.

findings.name: DLP notification rules not configured
findings.description: DLP policy violations are logged but no notification rules are configured. Security team is not alerted in real-time when sensitive data exfiltration is detected.
findings.recommendation: Configure DLP notification rules to alert the security team via email and SIEM integration. Set immediate alerts for critical DLP violations.

findings.name: DLP exact data match not configured for financial data
findings.description: Exact Data Match (EDM) is not configured for financial data such as credit card numbers, IBAN, and SWIFT codes. Pattern-based detection alone has higher false-positive rates.
findings.recommendation: Configure EDM profiles for structured financial data. Upload indexed datasets of credit card BINs and account number patterns for precise detection.

findings.name: Firewall rule allows outbound traffic on non-standard ports
findings.description: Default firewall policy allows outbound traffic on all ports. No restrictions on high-risk ports commonly used by C2 frameworks (4444, 8080, 8443, 1337).
findings.recommendation: Implement an explicit deny-all outbound firewall rule. Create allow rules only for required ports (80, 443, and business-specific ports).

findings.name: DNS tunneling detection not enabled
findings.description: DNS traffic is not inspected for tunneling activity. DNS tunneling is used for data exfiltration and C2 communication while evading traditional security controls.
findings.recommendation: Enable DNS tunneling detection in the cloud firewall. Configure alerts for anomalous DNS query patterns including high-entropy subdomains.

findings.name: IPS signatures not updated to latest version
findings.description: Cloud IPS is running signature version 2026.02 while the latest available is 2026.03. Missing 47 new detection signatures including recent zero-day exploit patterns.
findings.recommendation: Enable automatic IPS signature updates. Verify the update schedule is set to at least daily. Review and enable new signatures in detect or block mode.

findings.name: ZPA application segments using wildcard domains
findings.description: 3 application segments use wildcard domain patterns (*.internal.corp.com) exposing all subdomains through ZPA. This grants access to unintended internal applications.
findings.recommendation: Replace wildcard domain application segments with explicit FQDN entries. Enumerate specific applications and create individual segments with appropriate access policies.

findings.name: ZPA server groups with unhealthy connectors
findings.description: 2 server groups have connectors in unhealthy state for over 48 hours: 'DC-East-SG' (1 of 2 down) and 'AWS-Prod-SG' (2 of 3 down). Reduces redundancy and availability.
findings.recommendation: Investigate and restore unhealthy connectors. Ensure each server group has at minimum 2 healthy connectors. Set up connector health monitoring alerts.

findings.name: ZPA access policy allows broad user group access to sensitive apps
findings.description: Access policy 'Corporate-Access' grants the 'All Employees' group access to application segments including financial systems, HR applications, and admin consoles.
findings.recommendation: Implement least-privilege access policies. Create specific user groups for each application segment. Restrict sensitive application access to authorized teams only.

findings.name: ZPA posture profiles not enforced for high-security apps
findings.description: Device posture checks (OS version, disk encryption, AV status) are not enforced for access to 5 high-security application segments including finance and HR systems.
findings.recommendation: Create and enforce posture profiles requiring: current OS patches, disk encryption enabled, approved AV running, and domain-joined status for sensitive apps.

findings.name: Trusted network configuration includes overly broad IP ranges
findings.description: Trusted network 'Office-Networks' includes a /16 range (10.0.0.0/16) encompassing guest Wi-Fi, IoT networks, and visitor subnets alongside corporate endpoints.
findings.recommendation: Narrow trusted network definitions to specific corporate subnets. Exclude guest, IoT, and contractor networks from trusted ranges.

findings.name: Trusted network not enforcing Zscaler Client Connector
findings.description: When on trusted networks, Zscaler Client Connector tunneling is disabled. Users on the corporate network bypass Zscaler inspection entirely.
findings.recommendation: Enable ZCC tunnel even on trusted networks for consistent policy enforcement. Use location-aware policies rather than bypassing inspection.

findings.name: Admin portal MFA not enforced
findings.description: The Zscaler admin portal does not enforce multi-factor authentication for administrator logins. Compromised admin credentials would grant full policy control.
findings.recommendation: Enable MFA for all Zscaler admin portal accounts. Configure SAML SSO with the corporate IdP that enforces MFA for admin access.

findings.name: Excessive super admin accounts
findings.description: 8 accounts have super admin privileges in the Zscaler admin portal. Recommended maximum is 3 super admin accounts for break-glass scenarios.
findings.recommendation: Reduce super admin accounts to 3 or fewer. Assign granular admin roles (read-only, policy admin, report viewer) based on job function.

findings.name: Admin audit log retention set to 30 days
findings.description: Admin audit logs are retained for only 30 days. This is insufficient for compliance requirements and security investigation timelines.
findings.recommendation: Configure admin audit log export to SIEM or cloud storage with 12-month retention. Enable real-time log streaming for security monitoring.

findings.name: PAC file allows direct internet access for specific domains
findings.description: The PAC file configuration includes 43 domains for direct internet access bypassing Zscaler. Some bypassed domains include cloud services that should be inspected.
findings.recommendation: Review and minimize PAC file bypass entries. Move cloud service inspection to Zscaler inline policies. Remove stale or unnecessary bypass entries.

findings.name: GRE tunnel forwarding without redundancy
findings.description: 2 office locations forward traffic via single GRE tunnel without a backup tunnel. Tunnel failure would result in complete loss of internet access or falling back to direct access.
findings.recommendation: Configure primary and secondary GRE tunnels for each location. Implement automatic failover. Consider IPSec tunnels as backup.

findings.name: Web log streaming to SIEM not configured
findings.description: Nanolog Streaming Service (NSS) is not configured to forward web transaction logs to the corporate SIEM. Security event correlation with web traffic is not possible.
findings.recommendation: Configure NSS to stream web, firewall, and DNS logs to the SIEM. Enable log feeds for all transaction types including allowed and blocked.

findings.name: Sandbox analysis results not integrated with alerting
findings.description: Cloud Sandbox analysis results are available in the Zscaler dashboard but are not forwarded as alerts to the SOC. Malware detections may be missed.
findings.recommendation: Configure sandbox verdict notifications. Integrate sandbox results with SIEM alerting. Create automated incident workflows for confirmed malware verdicts.

findings.name: Bandwidth control not configured for streaming services
findings.description: No bandwidth throttling rules are configured for streaming video services (YouTube, Netflix, Twitch). These services consume disproportionate bandwidth.
findings.recommendation: Configure bandwidth control rules to limit streaming service bandwidth during business hours. Allow full bandwidth outside peak hours.

findings.name: Browser isolation not enabled for high-risk categories
findings.description: Cloud Browser Isolation is available but not configured for high-risk URL categories including newly registered domains, uncategorized sites, and webmail.
findings.recommendation: Enable Cloud Browser Isolation for high-risk URL categories. Configure isolation for email links and uncategorized URLs to prevent drive-by downloads.

findings.name: Shadow IT discovery not reviewing unsanctioned apps
findings.description: Shadow IT discovery has identified 156 unsanctioned cloud applications in use but no review workflow exists. Unsanctioned apps may lack security controls.
findings.recommendation: Establish a monthly shadow IT review process. Block high-risk unsanctioned apps. Create a self-service app request workflow for business-justified applications.

findings.name: CASB inline policies not blocking sensitive file uploads to personal cloud storage
findings.description: CASB inline policies monitor but do not block file uploads to personal instances of cloud storage (personal Google Drive, personal OneDrive).
findings.recommendation: Configure CASB tenant restrictions to differentiate corporate vs personal cloud storage instances. Block uploads to personal storage tenants.
